sourceMovie.title: So Close
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: So Close (2002) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Corey Yuen's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If So Close clicked for you, Kill Bill: Vol. 2 (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after So Close?
faq.answer: Try Kill Bill: Vol. 2, Wanted, The Transporter—each shares DNA with So Close while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is So Close?
faq.answer: So Close (2002) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is So Close worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
